title: NASA and Small Business Administration partner on funding key space technologies
summary: NASA is collaborating with the U.S. Small Business Administration to secure funding for companies developing essential space technologies. The partnership aims to attract investment in critical space components.

title: Airbus to build Aeolus-2 wind-monitoring satellite
summary: The European Space Agency has selected Airbus Defence and Space to develop the Aeolus-2 satellite, which will succeed the original Aeolus wind-monitoring mission. Airbus will lead the construction of this next-generation satellite.

title: GAO flags satellite costs, launch risks in Space Force portfolio
summary: The Government Accountability Office (GAO) has raised concerns about rising costs and workforce reductions for missile-warning satellites in the U.S. Space Force's portfolio. These issues could potentially delay national security launches due to gaps in digital engineering.

title: Pegasus launches Swift reboost mission
summary: A Pegasus XL rocket launched a mission to reboost NASA's Swift spacecraft, potentially marking the final flight of the Pegasus rocket. The launch was reported by SpaceNews.

title: Chinese satellite manufacturer Hongqing raises $191 million
summary: Hongqing Technology, a satellite manufacturing affiliate of Landspace, raised $191 million in a funding round, marking one of the largest single raises for a Chinese commercial satellite maker.

title: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies shifts focus to space
summary: Perovskite solar panel startup Verde Technologies is shifting its focus from rooftop applications to commercializing perovskite-based solar panels for space use. The company aims to power orbital data centers and large satellite constellations with its thin-film technology.

title: Isar Aerospace to launch German-built Planet imaging satellite
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a contract from Planet's German subsidiary to launch an imaging satellite, showcasing Germany's end-to-end space capabilities. This deal highlights Isar Aerospace's growing role in the European space industry.

title: Polish space tech company Sybilla Technologies secures funds to enter U.S. market
summary: Sybilla Technologies, a Polish space tech company, has secured a $10 million investment from BGK and 3TS Capital Partners to expand into the U.S. market. The funding will support the company's growth and international ambitions.

title: Making satellite communications more resilient
summary: The episode of Space Minds features a discussion with Kymeta’s Ryan Stevenson about improving satellite communications resilience through multi-orbit and multi-band connectivity in a single terminal. The conversation highlights the benefits and challenges of this approach.

title: Final Atlas 5 Amazon Leo mission launches
summary: The final Atlas 5 rocket launched on July 2, carrying a payload of Amazon's Leo satellites. This mission marks the end of the Atlas 5 vehicle's use for satellite deployments.

title: NASA tests advanced new Mars rover prototype in the California desert (video)
summary: NASA is testing a new Mars rover prototype in the California desert to develop robots capable of autonomous navigation in challenging terrain. The prototype aims to improve future rovers' ability to operate on the Moon or Mars without getting stuck.

title: America 250: From 1776 to the moon and beyond (A Space.com series)
summary: Space.com reflects on U.S. space achievements from 1776 to the present and speculates on future advancements as America celebrates its 250th anniversary.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 217 — America in Space
summary: The podcast episode discusses the progression of American space efforts. Rod Pyle and Tariq Malik analyze key developments in the U.S. space industry.

title: Human flight was still 7 years away in 1776. Now, we're headed back to the moon
summary: The article reflects on humanity's progress in flight since 1776, noting that human flight was still decades away at that time. It highlights the modern ambition to return to the moon.

title: What did the night sky look like on the 1st Independence Day 250 years ago?
summary: The news discusses what the night sky looked like on July 4, 1776, during the U.S. Independence Day. It connects this historical event to Benjamin Franklin and other colonists.

title: How to find Uranus this week, the hardest planet I've ever tried to see
summary: This week offers a rare opportunity to spot Uranus in the night sky, with Mars passing nearby as a helpful guide. The event is ideal for amateur astronomers looking to observe the seventh planet.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spies Stellar Sparkler for July 4th
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a stunning image of red, white, and blue stars resembling a sparkler. The image was released to celebrate the July 4th holiday.

title: NASA’s Hubble Captures Crimson Cloud Sparkling with White, Blue Stars
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of a crimson gas cloud illuminated by bright blue and white stars. The vibrant colors highlight the stellar activity within the nebula.

title: NASA’s Hubble Spots Star-Spangled Cosmic Scene
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured an image of the globular cluster Messier 3 (M3) featuring over 500,000 stars in red, white, and blue hues. This cosmic scene highlights the dense stellar population and vibrant colors of the cluster.

title: NASA’s Artemis II Breaks Agency Streaming Record
summary: NASA's Artemis II mission achieved record-breaking viewership with over 149.4 million views across its live coverage, including launch, lunar flyby, and splashdown. The mission demonstrated strong global public engagement through 24/7 NASA-owned streaming platforms.

title: Good Morning, Earth!
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams captured a photo of an orbital sunrise from the International Space Station on June 26, 2026. The ISS completes 16 orbits around Earth daily, experiencing 16 sunrises and sunsets.

title: NASA’s Webb Reveals Stars Sparking to Life in Cosmic Celebration
summary: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope captured infrared images of stars forming in the FS Tau star system, revealing previously unseen features behind thick dust. The image also showcased numerous background galaxies, adding to the cosmic spectacle.

title: What’s Up: July 2026 Skywatching Tips from NASA
summary: NASA shares skywatching tips for July 2026, highlighting a Moon-and-planets alignment, a comet's return, Milky Way visibility, and Saturn's rings at a unique angle.

title: NASA’s Chandra Examines Milky Way at Arms’ Length
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory reveals that the outer spiral arms of the Milky Way may extend wider than previously believed, prompting astronomers to reconsider the galaxy's structure. The discovery was made through precise measurements of distances to dust clouds.

title: NASA Seeks Volunteers for New Yearlong Simulated Moon, Mars Mission
summary: NASA is recruiting volunteers for a yearlong simulated mission to the Moon or Mars, starting in August 2027. The study aims to gather insights under isolated conditions at the Johnson Space Center.

title: LINK Spacecraft Set for Mission to Boost NASA’s Swift Observatory
summary: A robotic servicing spacecraft named LINK, developed by Katalyst Space, is scheduled to launch on July 2 to boost the orbit of NASA's Neil Gehrels Swift Observatory. The mission will take place from Kwajalein Atoll in the Republic of the Marshall Islands.

title: Vast’s Haven-1 continues to advance through milestones ahead of planned 2027 launch
summary: Vast Space's Haven-1 commercial space station is progressing toward its planned Q1 2027 launch, having achieved key milestones. The station aims to serve as a commercial destination for research and tourism.

title: Starship in Florida pushing for launch this year
summary: SpaceX is accelerating preparations for Starship launches from Cape Canaveral, aiming for a launch this year. The company and its contractors are making progress to meet this ambitious timeline.

title: NASA announces new lander contracts for Moon Base Phase One
summary: NASA announced four new lunar lander contracts as part of its Moon Base Phase One initiative in June 2026. The contracts aim to support the development of lunar landers for future Moon missions.

title: Blue Origin accelerates LC-36 recovery via hybrid plan
summary: Blue Origin is accelerating the recovery of Launch Complex-36 at Cape Canaveral using a hybrid plan following a recent anomaly that damaged the facility during the New Glenn launch. The company aims to restore operations swiftly after the incident.

title: Launch Preview: Swift rescue mission, final Atlas satellite launch
summary: A rescue mission for the Swift observatory is planned alongside the final launch of an Atlas satellite. Multiple orbital launches are scheduled globally this week.

title: Pegasus XL set to air launch Swift Boost Mission to save NASA space telescope
summary: A Northrop Grumman Pegasus XL rocket is scheduled for its first mission since 2021 to air launch the Swift Boost Mission, aiming to save a NASA space telescope. The mission highlights the ongoing use of air-launched rockets for space exploration.

title: Euclid studies Milky Way center, supports future Roman observations
summary: ESA's Euclid space telescope captured a new image of the Milky Way's center, providing data to aid future observations by NASA's Roman Space Telescope. The image highlights Euclid's capability to study dense star fields and support complementary missions.

title: Ship 40 completes single-engine static fire; Booster 20 and Pad 2 see new work
summary: SpaceX conducted a single-engine static fire test for Ship 40. Meanwhile, Booster 20 and Pad 2 are undergoing new work.

title: Space Shuttle Endeavour displayed vertically in new exhibit at California Science Center
summary: Space Shuttle Endeavour has been displayed vertically in a new exhibit at the California Science Center. The exhibit aims to educate the public about the shuttle's legacy after 25 flights.

title: Launch Preview: Pegasus XL to launch amongst Falcon 9 and Chinese missions
summary: This week will see six orbital launches, including the first flight of the Pegasus XL rocket alongside missions by Falcon 9 and Chinese launchers. The Pegasus XL launch is highlighted as a notable event in the global launch manifest.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The news provided is incomplete and lacks sufficient details to analyze. No specific space industry news is available for summarization.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary by releasing a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to present the follow-up to the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level 2025 (CM25), detailing planned activities, priorities, and industrial opportunities across its programs.

title: One robotic arm to rule them all
summary: The article discusses a new robotic arm designed to serve multiple purposes in the space industry. It highlights the versatility and potential applications of this technology.

title: We’re back: Proba-3 ready for more science
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft and ASPIICS instrument are fully operational, resuming formation flying to create artificial eclipses for scientific observations.

title: ESA’s Prodex programme brings scientific research to space
summary: ESA's Prodex programme has enabled the successful launch of the 4DSpace-Daedalus mission in Norway. The programme supports research institutes in participating in European space science activities and missions.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical center in the Netherlands, with an online attendance option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Inside the Deepening Ties Between Europe and the Middle East
summary: Europe's strained geopolitical ties with the US are fostering stronger space industry collaborations with Middle Eastern companies. This shift is creating new opportunities for partnerships in the space sector.

title: True Anomaly Approaches, Images Rocket Lab Craft
summary: True Anomaly completed a task to image a Rocket Lab craft within 61 hours of receiving the order, which was shorter than the 72-hour deadline set by SSC. The mission highlights True Anomaly's rapid response capabilities in space operations.

title: Exclusive: Austria Taps R-Space for Its Second Military Sat
summary: Austria has selected R-Space to build its second military satellite, Aurora. The mission is part of a series of government-backed demonstrations to test new orbital technologies.

title: Exclusive: Lunar Forge Unveils Plans to Build Lunar Infrastructure Out of Dust
summary: Lunar Forge plans to construct lunar infrastructure using regolith to reduce costs for fission reactors. The company aims to use lunar dust to build support structures and shielding.

title: NASA Praises Blue Origin’s Mishap Progress, Announces Moon Base Contracts
summary: NASA commended Blue Origin's progress in addressing past mishaps and awarded contracts for moon base missions. The agency aims for monthly lunar landings using updated lander designs.

title: Payload Field Guide: CLPS Missions
summary: The article discusses CLPS (Commercial Lunar Payload Services) missions aimed at landing on the Moon. It serves as a guide to these upcoming lunar missions.

title: Exclusive: Satellogic Expands AI Apps with SpaceKnow Partnership
summary: Satellogic partnered with SpaceKnow to develop AI applications using Satellogic's satellite imagery. This collaboration aims to enhance geospatial intelligence analysis capabilities.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Satcom Market With $8B Iridium Deal
summary: Rocket Lab has entered the satcom market through an $8 billion deal with Iridium. This marks a significant expansion for the company into space applications.

title: Firefly Targets Swedish Launch in 2028 Under New Franchise Model
summary: Firefly Aerospace plans to launch its Alpha rocket from Esrange Space Center in Sweden by 2028 under a new franchise model. The collaboration with SSC Space marks Firefly's expansion into international markets.

title: US Army Establishes a Space Operations Branch
summary: The US Army has established a new Space Operations Branch to better integrate space-based technologies into its military operations. This move highlights the growing importance of space capabilities in modern warfare.

title: Amazon Leo Constellation Ready to Support Initial Service After ULA Mission
summary: Amazon's LEO constellation has achieved enough satellites in orbit to support initial service after a ULA launch. This marks a key milestone for Amazon's satellite internet ambitions.

title: Isar Aerospace Lands Big Win With Planet Launch Contract
summary: Isar Aerospace secured a launch contract with Planet to launch one of Planet’s next-generation Pelican satellites, potentially as early as late 2026. The agreement marks a significant milestone for Isar Aerospace’s developing launcher.

title: Mitsubishi Electric to Acquire Infostellar
summary: Mitsubishi Electric has announced its intention to acquire Infostellar, a prominent company in the Japanese space sector. The acquisition marks a significant move in the industry.

title: Vodafone Ireland Conducts Emergency Services D2D Call With AST SpaceMobile JV
summary: Vodafone Ireland successfully conducted a test call with AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation to evaluate connectivity for emergency and first responders. The test is part of ongoing efforts to enhance emergency services communication.

title: The Internet in Space: The Apps that Dominate Satellite Traffic and Why it Matters
summary: The article discusses the growing importance of satellite networks for non-government applications and the types of apps dominating satellite traffic as thousands of new satellites are launched into orbit. It explores the practical uses of satellite internet beyond traditional government roles.

title: Dish Satellite TV and Wireless Businesses File for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: Dish Network's satellite TV and wireless businesses filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy to facilitate the transition of its wireless operations after spectrum transactions. The move aims to streamline the company's financial restructuring process.

title: Ovzon Wins New Defense Deal with European NATO Nation
summary: Ovzon secured a $7.1 million defense contract with a European NATO nation for its satellite communications solution. The deal is valued at 74 million Swedish krona.

title: Unseenlabs Orders 2 Cubesats from GomSpace
summary: French radio frequency detection company Unseenlabs ordered two additional cubesats from smallsat manufacturer GomSpace for a contract worth 2.2 million euros ($2.5 million). The deal was announced by GomSpace on Wednesday.

title: Lite Coms Hires Calspan Exec Greg Campbell as President
summary: Lite Coms has appointed Greg Campbell, a former Calspan executive, as its new president. Campbell will lead the company's daily operations and focus on expanding its manufacturing capabilities.

title: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines Win NASA Lunar Delivery Missions
summary: Astrobotic, Firefly Aerospace, and Intuitive Machines have secured NASA contracts to deliver payloads to the Moon as part of efforts to establish a lunar base. This marks a significant step in NASA's lunar exploration and commercial partnerships.
